title: galium boreale subsp. septentrionale
intro: Galium boreale subsp. septentrionale, also known as northern bedstraw or northern tick trefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to be used as a diuretic and to treat various kidney and urinary issues due to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Galium boreale subsp. septentrionale include reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various conditions. Galium boreale subsp. septentrionale can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
